The combination of deciduous hardwood forests, wide asphalt roads without curbs in residential areas, and specific wooden utility pole construction is highly characteristic of the Northeastern US. The architectural style of the houses and the lush, temperate greenery are consistent with New England geography.

The landscape, vegetation, and climate indicators are nearly identical to the Northeastern US, particularly in provinces like Nova Scotia or New Brunswick. It is geographically indistinguishable from the US Northeast based solely on terrain and flora.

The vegetation, specifically the maple species and the general deciduous forest composition, is virtually identical to the Northeastern US. Southern Ontario and the Maritime provinces share this exact botanical profile.
